content_en:
Q. 27. What is the ruling on wearing amulets and talismans?

A. This issue, I mean the wearing of talismans and amulets is divided into two categories:

"1 See the previous question.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 100 PHY OI oot

1. The thing worn is from the Qur’an and the scholars both the early generations and the later generations have disagreed regarding this. Some of them permitted it, considering that it is included in the Words of Allah, the Most High:

% 8 Bee, yee chee cede ede

“And We send down of the Quran that which is a healing and a mercy to those who believe (in Islamic Monotheism and act on in)

and the Words of Him, the Most High: “(This is) a Book (the Qur’an) which We have sent down to you, full of blessings.’"!

And it is from its blessings, that it is worn to protect against evil.

There are those who forbid it, saying: It has not been confirmed from the Prophet #% that wearing them is a lawful means of repelling evil or removing it. And, the fundamental principle in matters such as these is to refrain from them, and this is the most authoritative view. So, it is not permissible to wear talismans, even if they are from the Noble Quran, nor is it permissible to place them under the pillow of the sick person, or to hang them on the wall or the like. Supplication and recitation may only be made directly over him, as the Prophet # used to do.

2. The thing worn is from something other than the Noble Qur’4n, whose meaning is not understood. This is not permissible in any condition, because what is written is not known, and some people write charms and complicated things, interwoven letters, that are almost impossible to understand or to read. Such things are forbidden innovations, and they are not

allowed in any circumstance. And Allah knows better.

Q. 28. Is it permissible to write some Qur’anic Verses (such as Ayat Al-Kursi) upon eating and drinking vessels in order to treat oneself?

A. We should know that the Book of Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful is more mighty and powerful than to be abused in this manner, or to be degraded in this manner. How can a believer be happy to place the Book of Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful and the greatest Verse in the Book of Allah, which is Ayat Al-Kursi to place it in a vessel from which he drinks where it is abused and tossed around in the house, and where the children play with it?! There is no doubt this act is forbidden and that it is an obligation upon anyone who has such vessels to remove these Verses which are in them by going to a craftsman who can erase them. If this not possible, then it is an obligation upon him to dig a hole for them in a clean place and bury them. But that he should continue to allow them to be degraded and abused by the children drinking from them and playing with them (he should know that) seeking a cure in this manner has not been reported from the righteous Salaf, may Allah be pleased with them.

Q. 29. In some countries, the students in Islamic schools learn that the way of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah is: Faith in the Names of Allah, the Most High and His Attributes, without corrupting the meaning, without denial, without explaining how and without comparison. And is the division of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah into two categories (i) the school of Ibn Taymiyyah and his students and (ii) the schools of Al-Asha‘irah"! and

"1 ALAsha‘irah: Named after Abul-Hasan Al-Ash‘ari. Its adherants deny most of the Attributes of Allah, indulging in theological rhetoric and

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 102 PLY ots yi cyt

the Maturidiyyah"! a correct division? And what is the view of the Muslim regarding the scholars who make Ta’wil'?! (of Qur’anic Verses and Prophetic Ahadith)?

A. There is no doubt that what is learnt in the schools, that the way of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah is belief in the Names and Attributes of Allah, the Most High, without corrupting the meaning, without denial, without explaining how and without comparison; that agrees with what has been reported in the Sunnah, according to Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah as proven by their books, both detailed and summarized, and it is the truth which conforms to what is said in the Book (of Allah), the Sunnah and the sayings of the Salaf. This also conforms with correct thinking and the rational mind. It is not our intention to enumerate the individual evidences for this, as this was not requested in the question; we are only answering what was asked, and that is the division of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah into two groups in two schools:

One of Them: The school of Ibn Taymiyyah and his students who forbade explaining the evidences (of the Qur’an and Sunnah) with meanings other than their obvious ones.

The Other: The school of the Asha‘irah and the Maturidiyyah who require that the Names of Allah and His Attributes be explained with meanings other than their obvious ones.

We say: It is well known that between these two schools there

debate. Ironically, Abul-Hasan refuted his earlier beliefs before he died and embraced the beliefs of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah (Ref. his final work, Al-Ibanah)

"1 4l-Maturidiyyah: A school of thought named after Abu Mansur Muhammad Al-Maturidi (d. 944 C. E.). The Maturidiyyah creed is similar to that of Al-Asha‘irah.

'?] Tq’wil: Interpretation, when it refers to an incorrect interpretation, it means to alter the meaning in order to conform to one’s own beliefs.

is a clear differing in their ways of understanding matters related to the Names and Attributes of Allah: The teachers of the first school affirm the obligation to understand the evidences according to their obvious meanings in matters related to the Names and Attributes of Allah at the same time negating what must be negated regarding Allah, the Most High, such as comparison and explaining how. And the teachers of the second school affirm the obligation to change the meanings of the evidences regarding the Names and Attributes of Allah. And these two paths differ completely and their differing is clear from the following example:

Allah, the Most High says:

IS GS 3 Ges be 1 Le “Nay, both His Hands are widely outstretched. He spends (of His bounty) as He wills.“""} And He says, reporting His rebuke of Iblis when he refused to prostrate to Adam, peace be upon him, in accordance with Allah’s Command:

“(Allah) said: 0 Iblis ( Satan)! What prevents you from prostrating yourself to one whom I have created with Both My Hands.""?! And the teachers of the two schools have differed regarding the meaning of the two Hands which Allah, the Most High has confirmed for Himself.

Those belonging to the first school said: The apparent meaning must remain, and the existence of two real Hands must be confirmed for Allah, in a manner befitting Him.

Faiawa Arkanul-Islam 104 PEM OS yt cath

Those belonging to the second school said: The obvious meaning must be changed and it is forbidden to confirm the existence of real Hands for Allah, the Most High. Then they differed as to whether their meaning is Strength or Blessings.

From this example, it is clear that the paths of the two schools are different and incompatible and it is not possible to combine them both in one attribute, which is Ahlus-Sunnah.

Therefore, the description of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah must belong to only one of them, and not the other, and we will judge between them with fairness, and we will submit them both to the measure of impartiality which is the Book of Allah and the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah #% and the sayings of the Companions, may Allah be pleased with them, and those who faithfully followed them among the Salaf of the (Muslim) community and their Imams.

There is nothing in this measure of impartiality which proves in any manner whatsoever, either open or implied, or which is clearly understood, nor is there any indication in that, of what the second school claims. Rather, in this measure (i.e. the Qur’an and the Sunnah) there is evidence that is clear, obvious and indicative for what the first school claims. Accordingly, it is determined that the attribute of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah belongs exclusively to them, and the second school does not share in it with them. Because the ruling of their sharing in it with them is unjust and an attempt to reconcile two opposites; and injustice is forbidden by the Islamic Law, and reconciling two opposites is rejected by logic.

As for the claim of the second school (those who make Ta‘wil): It is that there is no objection to making Ta’wil of the Names and Attributes of Allah, as long as it does not contradict any legal evidence.

We say: Simply changing the meaning of the words from their apparent one, without any lawful evidence contradicts the

Fatawa on ‘aqeedah (Creed) 105 Baal! Syl

evidence, and to do so is to speak of Allah, the Most High without knowledge, and Allah, the Most High has forbidden that in His Words:

2 Oy ee Hb Seidl SE

“Say (O Muhammad 3%): (But) the things that my Lord has indeed forbidden are Al-Fawahish (great evil sins and every kind of unlawful sexual intercourse) whether committed openly or secretly, sins (of all kinds), unrighteous oppression, joining partners (in worship) with Allah for which He has given no authority, and saying things about Allah of which you have no knowledge.’

5 AES BBG sal Cat thy BAG Ep

‘And follow not (O man, i.e., say not or do not or witness not) that

of which you have no knowledge. Verily, the hearing, and the

sight, and the heart of each of those ones will be questioned (by

Allah).’") And those who corrupt the meanings of Allah, the Most High’s Names and Attributes have no knowledge transmitted to them about those things whose meanings they change. Nor do they have any rational theory, except doubts which they cite as evidence, some of the things contradict others and necessitate an attribution of imperfection in Allah’s Nature and in His Attributes and His Revelation, which is greater than the imperfections which they claim would result from confirming them in their obvious meanings, and this is not the place to speak extensively about that.

"1 ALA taf 7:33 1 ALIsra’ 17:36

All that is intended is to make it clear that the description of Ahlus-Sunnah may not be given to two groups which differ completely in their way of thinking; the only one which deserves it is the one whose sayings agree with the Sunnah; and there can be no doubt to anyone who examines these beliefs with knowledge and impartiality that those who belong to the first school (not those who make Ta‘wil) have more right to bear the aforementioned description than those of the second school (those who make Ta’wil), therefore, it is not correct to divide Ahlus-Sunnah into two categories; rather they are one group.
content_id:
T. 27. Apa hukum memakai jimat dan tamimah?

J. Masalah ini, yaitu memakai tamimah dan jimat, terbagi menjadi dua kategori:

"1 Lihat pertanyaan sebelumnya.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 100 PHY OI oot

1. Benda yang dipakai berasal dari Al-Quran, dan para ulama baik dari generasi awal maupun generasi belakangan telah berselisih pendapat mengenai hal ini. Sebagian dari mereka membolehkannya, dengan menganggap bahwa hal itu termasuk dalam firman Allah Ta'ala:

% 8 Bee, yee chee cede ede

"Dan Kami turunkan dari Al-Quran apa yang menjadi penyembuh dan rahmat bagi orang-orang yang beriman (kepada tawhid dan mengamalkannya)"

dan firman-Nya Ta'ala: "(Ini adalah) sebuah Kitab (Al-Quran) yang Kami turunkan kepadamu, penuh dengan keberkahan."

Dan termasuk dari keberkahan Al-Quran adalah memakainya untuk perlindungan dari kejahatan.

Ada pula ulama yang melarangnya, dengan mengatakan: Tidak ada riwayat yang sahih dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa memakainya merupakan sarana yang disyariatkan untuk menolak kejahatan atau menghilangkannya. Dan prinsip dasar dalam perkara seperti ini adalah menahan diri darinya, dan inilah pendapat yang paling kuat. Maka, tidak boleh memakai tamimah, meskipun berasal dari Al-Quran yang mulia, dan tidak boleh pula meletakkannya di bawah bantal orang sakit, atau menggantungnya di dinding dan semisalnya. Du'a dan bacaan hanya boleh dibacakan secara langsung kepadanya, sebagaimana yang dilakukan Nabi ﷺ.

2. Benda yang dipakai berasal dari selain Al-Quran yang mulia, yang maknanya tidak dipahami. Ini tidak diperbolehkan dalam keadaan apa pun, karena apa yang tertulis tidak diketahui maknanya, dan sebagian orang menulis jampi-jampi dan hal-hal yang rumit, huruf-huruf yang saling bertautan, yang hampir mustahil untuk dipahami atau dibaca. Hal-hal semacam ini adalah bid'ah yang terlarang, dan tidak diperbolehkan dalam keadaan apa pun. Wallahu a'lam.

T. 28. Apakah diperbolehkan menulis beberapa ayat Al-Quran (seperti Ayat Al-Kursi) pada wadah makan dan minum untuk tujuan pengobatan?

J. Kita harus mengetahui bahwa Kitab Allah 'Azza wa Jalla lebih agung dan mulia daripada diperlakukan dengan cara seperti ini, atau dihinakan dengan cara seperti ini. Bagaimana mungkin seorang mukmin merasa senang meletakkan Kitab Allah 'Azza wa Jalla dan ayat terbesar dalam Kitab Allah, yaitu Ayat Al-Kursi, meletakkannya dalam wadah yang darinya ia minum, di mana ia diperlakukan tidak semestinya dan dilempar-lempar di dalam rumah, dan di mana anak-anak bermain dengannya?! Tidak diragukan lagi bahwa perbuatan ini haram dan merupakan kewajiban bagi siapa saja yang memiliki wadah semacam itu untuk menghapus ayat-ayat yang ada di dalamnya dengan membawanya kepada tukang yang dapat menghapusnya. Jika hal itu tidak memungkinkan, maka wajib baginya untuk menggali lubang di tempat yang bersih dan menguburnya. Adapun membiarkannya terus dihinakan dan diperlakukan tidak semestinya oleh anak-anak yang minum darinya dan bermain dengannya, (hendaklah ia tahu bahwa) mencari kesembuhan dengan cara seperti ini tidak pernah diriwayatkan dari para Salaf yang saleh, semoga Allah meridhai mereka.

T. 29. Di beberapa negara, para pelajar di sekolah-sekolah Islam mempelajari bahwa jalan Ahlus Sunnah wal Jama'ah adalah: Beriman kepada Nama-Nama Allah Ta'ala dan Sifat-Sifat-Nya, tanpa mengubah makna, tanpa mengingkari, tanpa menjelaskan caranya, dan tanpa menyerupakan. Apakah pembagian Ahlus Sunnah wal Jama'ah menjadi dua kelompok: (i) madzhab Ibnu Taimiyyah dan murid-muridnya dan (ii) madzhab Al-Asya'irah dan

Al-Asya'irah: Dinamakan menurut Abul Hasan Al-Asy'ari. Para pengikutnya mengingkari sebagian besar Sifat-Sifat Allah, tenggelam dalam perdebatan teologis dan

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 102 PLY ots yi cyt

Al-Maturidiyyah merupakan pembagian yang benar? Dan bagaimana pandangan seorang Muslim terhadap para ulama yang melakukan ta'wil (terhadap ayat-ayat Al-Quran dan hadith-hadith Nabi)?

J. Tidak diragukan lagi bahwa apa yang dipelajari di sekolah-sekolah, bahwa jalan Ahlus Sunnah wal Jama'ah adalah beriman kepada Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat Allah Ta'ala, tanpa mengubah makna, tanpa mengingkari, tanpa menjelaskan caranya, dan tanpa menyerupakan; hal itu sesuai dengan apa yang diriwayatkan dalam Sunnah, menurut Ahlus Sunnah wal Jama'ah sebagaimana dibuktikan oleh kitab-kitab mereka, baik yang terperinci maupun yang ringkas, dan itulah kebenaran yang sesuai dengan apa yang disebutkan dalam Kitab (Allah), Sunnah, dan perkataan para Salaf. Hal ini juga sesuai dengan pemikiran yang benar dan akal yang sehat. Bukan tujuan kami untuk menyebutkan dalil-dalil secara individual, karena hal itu tidak diminta dalam pertanyaan; kami hanya menjawab apa yang ditanyakan, yaitu pembagian Ahlus Sunnah wal Jama'ah menjadi dua kelompok dalam dua madzhab:

Yang Pertama: Madzhab Ibnu Taimiyyah dan murid-muridnya yang melarang menafsirkan dalil-dalil (Al-Quran dan Sunnah) dengan makna selain makna lahiriahnya.

Yang Kedua: Madzhab Al-Asya'irah dan Al-Maturidiyyah yang mengharuskan Nama-Nama Allah dan Sifat-Sifat-Nya ditafsirkan dengan makna selain makna lahiriahnya.

Kami katakan: Sudah diketahui bahwa antara kedua madzhab ini terdapat

perdebatan. Ironisnya, Abul Hasan membantah keyakinannya yang terdahulu sebelum beliau wafat dan memeluk keyakinan Ahlus Sunnah wal Jama'ah (Ref. karya terakhir beliau, Al-Ibanah)

Al-Maturidiyyah: Sebuah aliran pemikiran yang dinamakan menurut Abu Manshur Muhammad Al-Maturidi (w. 944 M). Akidah Al-Maturidiyyah serupa dengan akidah Al-Asya'irah.

Ta'wil: Penafsiran; ketika merujuk pada penafsiran yang salah, artinya mengubah makna agar sesuai dengan keyakinan sendiri.

perbedaan yang jelas dalam cara mereka memahami perkara-perkara yang berkaitan dengan Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat Allah: Para guru dari madzhab pertama menegaskan kewajiban memahami dalil-dalil sesuai makna lahiriahnya dalam perkara-perkara yang berkaitan dengan Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat Allah, sekaligus menafikan apa yang harus dinafikan dari Allah Ta'ala, seperti penyerupaan dan penjelasan tentang caranya. Dan para guru dari madzhab kedua menegaskan kewajiban mengubah makna dalil-dalil mengenai Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat Allah. Dan kedua jalan ini berbeda sama sekali, dan perbedaan mereka jelas dari contoh berikut:

Allah Ta'ala berfirman:

IS GS 3 Ges be 1 Le "Bahkan kedua Tangan-Nya terbentang luas. Dia memberikan (karunia-Nya) sebagaimana Dia kehendaki." Dan Dia berfirman, mengabarkan teguran-Nya kepada Iblis ketika ia menolak bersujud kepada Adam 'alaihissalam, sesuai dengan perintah Allah:

"(Allah) berfirman: 'Wahai Iblis (setan)! Apa yang menghalangimu untuk bersujud kepada makhluk yang telah Aku ciptakan dengan kedua Tangan-Ku.'" Dan para guru dari kedua madzhab tersebut telah berselisih pendapat mengenai makna dua Tangan yang telah Allah Ta'ala tetapkan bagi Diri-Nya.

Mereka yang termasuk madzhab pertama berkata: Makna lahiriah harus tetap dipertahankan, dan keberadaan dua Tangan yang hakiki harus ditetapkan bagi Allah, dengan cara yang sesuai dengan keagungan-Nya.

Faiawa Arkanul-Islam 104 PEM OS yt cath

Mereka yang termasuk madzhab kedua berkata: Makna lahiriah harus diubah dan dilarang menetapkan keberadaan Tangan yang hakiki bagi Allah Ta'ala. Kemudian mereka berselisih apakah maknanya adalah Kekuatan atau Nikmat.

Dari contoh ini, jelaslah bahwa jalan kedua madzhab tersebut berbeda dan tidak dapat disatukan, serta tidak mungkin menggabungkan keduanya dalam satu sifat, yaitu Ahlus Sunnah.

Oleh karena itu, sifat Ahlus Sunnah wal Jama'ah harus dimiliki oleh salah satu dari keduanya saja, dan bukan yang lain, dan kami akan menghakimi antara keduanya dengan adil, dan kami akan menyerahkan keduanya kepada timbangan keadilan, yaitu Kitab Allah dan Sunnah Rasulullah ﷺ serta perkataan para Sahabat radhiyallahu 'anhum dan orang-orang yang mengikuti mereka dengan setia di antara para Salaf umat (Islam) ini dan para imam mereka.

Tidak ada satu pun dalam timbangan keadilan ini yang membuktikan dengan cara apa pun, baik secara terang-terangan maupun tersirat, atau yang dipahami secara jelas, dan tidak ada pula petunjuk di dalamnya, tentang apa yang diklaim oleh madzhab kedua. Bahkan, dalam timbangan ini (yaitu Al-Quran dan Sunnah) terdapat dalil yang jelas, nyata, dan menunjukkan apa yang diklaim oleh madzhab pertama. Karenanya, ditetapkan bahwa sifat Ahlus Sunnah wal Jama'ah hanya milik mereka secara eksklusif, dan madzhab kedua tidak bersekutu dengan mereka di dalamnya. Karena menetapkan bahwa mereka bersekutu di dalamnya adalah kezaliman dan upaya mendamaikan dua hal yang bertentangan; dan kezaliman dilarang oleh syariat Islam, sementara mendamaikan dua hal yang bertentangan ditolak oleh akal.

Adapun klaim madzhab kedua (mereka yang melakukan ta'wil): Bahwa tidak ada keberatan dalam melakukan ta'wil terhadap Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat Allah, selama hal itu tidak bertentangan dengan dalil syar'i mana pun.

Kami katakan: Sekadar mengubah makna kata-kata dari makna lahiriahnya, tanpa dalil syar'i apa pun, bertentangan dengan

Fatawa tentang 'aqeedah (Akidah) 105 Baal! Syl

dalil, dan melakukan hal tersebut berarti berbicara tentang Allah Yang Maha Tinggi tanpa ilmu, dan Allah Yang Maha Tinggi telah melarang hal itu dalam firman-Nya:

2 Oy ee Hb Seidl SE

"Katakanlah (wahai Muhammad ﷺ): (Tetapi) hal-hal yang benar-benar diharamkan oleh Rabbku adalah Al-Fawahish (dosa-dosa besar dan segala bentuk hubungan seksual yang tidak halal) baik yang dilakukan secara terang-terangan maupun tersembunyi, dosa-dosa (dari segala jenis), kezaliman, menyekutukan (dalam ibadah) dengan Allah sesuatu yang Dia tidak memberikan kewenangan atasnya, dan mengatakan hal-hal tentang Allah yang kalian tidak memiliki ilmu tentangnya.'

5 AES BBG sal Cat thy BAG Ep

'Dan janganlah kamu mengikuti (wahai manusia, yakni jangan mengatakan atau melakukan atau menyaksikan) sesuatu

yang kamu tidak memiliki ilmu tentangnya. Sesungguhnya pendengaran,

penglihatan, dan hati, masing-masing dari semua itu akan dimintai

pertanggungjawaban (oleh Allah).'" Dan mereka yang merusak makna Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat Allah Yang Maha Tinggi tidak memiliki ilmu yang diriwayatkan kepada mereka tentang hal-hal yang maknanya mereka ubah. Mereka juga tidak memiliki teori rasional apa pun, kecuali keraguan-keraguan yang mereka jadikan sebagai dalil, di mana sebagian hal tersebut bertentangan dengan yang lain dan mengharuskan penisbatan kekurangan pada Dzat Allah, pada Sifat-Sifat-Nya, dan pada Wahyu-Nya, yang lebih besar daripada kekurangan yang mereka klaim akan timbul dari menetapkannya sesuai makna zhahirnya, dan ini bukan tempat untuk membahas panjang lebar tentang hal tersebut.

"1 ALA taf 7:33 1 ALIsra' 17:36

Semua yang dimaksudkan di sini adalah untuk menjelaskan bahwa sebutan Ahlus-Sunnah tidak boleh diberikan kepada dua kelompok yang sama sekali berbeda dalam cara berpikir mereka; satu-satunya yang berhak menyandangnya adalah kelompok yang perkataannya sesuai dengan Sunnah; dan tidak ada keraguan bagi siapa pun yang mengkaji keyakinan-keyakinan ini dengan ilmu dan sikap netral bahwa mereka yang termasuk dalam madzhab pertama (bukan mereka yang melakukan Ta'wil) lebih berhak menyandang sebutan tersebut dibandingkan mereka dari madzhab kedua (mereka yang melakukan Ta'wil), oleh karena itu, tidaklah benar membagi Ahlus-Sunnah menjadi dua kategori; melainkan mereka adalah satu kelompok.
content_ar: Q. 27. What is the ruling on wearing amulets and talismans? A. This issue, I mean the wearing of talismans and amulets is divided into two categories: "1 See the previous question. الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 100 PHY OI oot 1. The thing worn is from the Qur’an and the العلماء both the early generations and the later generations have disagreed regarding this. Some of them permitted it, considering that it is included in the Words of الله, the Most High: % 8 Bee, yee chee cede ede “And We send down of القرآن الكريم that which is a healing and a mercy to those who believe (in الإسلامي التوحيد and act on in) and the Words of Him, the Most High: “(This is) a Book (the Qur’an) which We have sent down to you, full of blessings.’"! And it is from its blessings, that it is worn to protect against evil. There are those who forbid it, saying: It has not been confirmed from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% that wearing them is a lawful means of repelling evil or removing it. And, the fundamental principle in matters such as these is to refrain from them, and this is the most authoritative view. So, it is not مباح to wear talismans, even if they are from the Noble القرآن الكريم, nor is it مباح to place them under the pillow of the sick person, or to hang them on the wall or the like. الدعاء and recitation may only be made directly over him, as النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # used to do. 2. The thing worn is from something other than the Noble Qur’4n, whose meaning is not understood. This is not مباح in any condition, because what is written is not known, and some people write charms and complicated things, interwoven letters, that are almost impossible to understand or to read. Such things are محرم innovations, and they are not allowed in any circumstance. And الله knows better. Q. 28. Is it مباح to write some Qur’anic Verses (such as Ayat Al-Kursi) upon eating and drinking vessels in order to treat oneself? A. We should know that the Book of الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful is more mighty and powerful than to be abused in this manner, or to be degraded in this manner. How can a believer be happy to place the Book of الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful and the greatest Verse in the Book of الله, which is Ayat Al-Kursi to place it in a vessel from which he drinks where it is abused and tossed around in the house, and where the children play with it?! There is no doubt this act is محرم and that it is an obligation upon anyone who has such vessels to remove these Verses which are in them by going to a craftsman who can erase them. If this not possible, then it is an obligation upon him to dig a hole for them in a clean place and bury them. But that he should continue to allow them to be degraded and abused by the children drinking from them and playing with them (he should know that) seeking a cure in this manner has not been reported from the righteous Salaf, رضي الله عنهم. Q. 29. In some countries, the students in الإسلامي schools learn that the way of Ahlus-السنة wal-Jama‘ah is: الإيمان in the Names of الله, the Most High and His Attributes, without corrupting the meaning, without denial, without explaining how and without comparison. And is the division of Ahlus-السنة wal-Jama‘ah into two categories (i) the school of ابن تيمية and his students and (ii) the schools of Al-Asha‘irah"! and "1 ALAsha‘irah: Named after Abul-Hasan Al-Ash‘ari. Its adherants deny most of the Attributes of الله, indulging in theological rhetoric and الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 102 PLY ots yi cyt the Maturidiyyah"! a correct division? And what is the view of the المسلم regarding the العلماء who make Ta’wil'?! (of Qur’anic Verses and Prophetic Ahadith)? A. There is no doubt that what is learnt in the schools, that the way of Ahlus-السنة wal-Jama‘ah is belief in the Names and Attributes of الله, the Most High, without corrupting the meaning, without denial, without explaining how and without comparison; that agrees with what has been reported in السنة النبوية, according to Ahlus-السنة wal-Jama‘ah as proven by their books, both detailed and summarized, and it is the truth which conforms to what is said in the Book (of الله), السنة النبوية and the sayings of the Salaf. This also conforms with correct thinking and the rational mind. It is not our intention to enumerate the individual evidences for this, as this was not requested in the question; we are only answering what was asked, and that is the division of Ahlus-السنة wal-Jama‘ah into two groups in two schools: One of Them: The school of ابن تيمية and his students who forbade explaining the evidences (of the Qur’an and السنة) with meanings other than their obvious ones. The Other: The school of the Asha‘irah and the Maturidiyyah who require that the Names of الله and His Attributes be explained with meanings other than their obvious ones. We say: It is well known that between these two schools there debate. Ironically, Abul-Hasan refuted his earlier beliefs before he died and embraced the beliefs of Ahlus-السنة wal-Jama‘ah (Ref. his final work, Al-Ibanah) "1 4l-Maturidiyyah: A المذهب الفقهي named after Abu Mansur Muhammad Al-Maturidi (d. 944 C. E.). The Maturidiyyah creed is similar to that of Al-Asha‘irah. '?] Tq’wil: Interpretation, when it refers to an incorrect interpretation, it means to alter the meaning in order to conform to one’s own beliefs. is a clear differing in their ways of understanding matters related to the Names and Attributes of الله: The teachers of the first school affirm the obligation to understand the evidences according to their obvious meanings in matters related to the Names and Attributes of الله at the same time negating what must be negated regarding الله, the Most High, such as comparison and explaining how. And the teachers of the second school affirm the obligation to change the meanings of the evidences regarding the Names and Attributes of الله. And these two paths differ completely and their differing is clear from the following example: الله, the Most High says: IS GS 3 Ges be 1 Le “Nay, both His Hands are widely outstretched. He spends (of His bounty) as He wills.“""} And He says, reporting His rebuke of Iblis when he refused to prostrate to Adam, peace be upon him, in accordance with الله’s Command: “(الله) said: 0 Iblis ( Satan)! What prevents you from prostrating yourself to one whom I have created with Both My Hands.""?! And the teachers of the two schools have differed regarding the meaning of the two Hands which الله, the Most High has confirmed for Himself. Those belonging to the first school said: The apparent meaning must remain, and the existence of two real Hands must be confirmed for الله, in a manner befitting Him. Faiawa Arkanul-الإسلام 104 PEM OS yt cath Those belonging to the second school said: The obvious meaning must be changed and it is محرم to confirm the existence of real Hands for الله, the Most High. Then they differed as to whether their meaning is Strength or Blessings. From this example, it is clear that the paths of the two schools are different and incompatible and it is not possible to combine them both in one attribute, which is Ahlus-السنة. Therefore, the description of Ahlus-السنة wal-Jama‘ah must belong to only one of them, and not the other, and we will judge between them with fairness, and we will submit them both to the measure of impartiality which is the Book of الله and السنة النبوية of رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم #% and the sayings of الصحابة الكرام رضي الله عنهم, رضي الله عنهم, and those who faithfully followed them among the Salaf of the (المسلم) community and their الأئمة. There is nothing in this measure of impartiality which proves in any manner whatsoever, either open or implied, or which is clearly understood, nor is there any indication in that, of what the second school claims. Rather, in this measure (i.e. the Qur’an and السنة النبوية) there is evidence that is clear, obvious and indicative for what the first school claims. Accordingly, it is determined that the attribute of Ahlus-السنة wal-Jama‘ah belongs exclusively to them, and the second school does not share in it with them. Because the ruling of their sharing in it with them is unjust and an attempt to reconcile two opposites; and injustice is محرم by the الإسلامي Law, and reconciling two opposites is rejected by logic. As for the claim of the second school (those who make Ta‘wil): It is that there is no objection to making Ta’wil of the Names and Attributes of الله, as long as it does not contradict any legal evidence. We say: Simply changing the meaning of the words from their apparent one, without any lawful evidence contradicts the الفتاوى on ‘aqeedah (Creed) 105 Baal! Syl evidence, and to do so is to speak of الله, the Most High without knowledge, and الله, the Most High has محرم that in His Words: 2 Oy ee Hb Seidl SE “Say (O Muhammad 3%): (But) the things that my الرب has indeed محرم are Al-Fawahish (great evil sins and every kind of unlawful sexual intercourse) whether committed openly or secretly, sins (of all kinds), unrighteous oppression, joining partners (in worship) with الله for which He has given no authority, and saying things about الله of which you have no knowledge.’ 5 AES BBG sal Cat thy BAG Ep ‘And follow not (O man, i.e., say not or do not or witness not) that of which you have no knowledge. Verily, the hearing, and the sight, and the heart of each of those ones will be questioned (by الله).’") And those who corrupt the meanings of الله, the Most High’s Names and Attributes have no knowledge نقل to them about those things whose meanings they change. Nor do they have any rational theory, except doubts which they cite as evidence, some of the things contradict others and necessitate an attribution of imperfection in الله’s Nature and in His Attributes and His Revelation, which is greater than the imperfections which they claim would result from confirming them in their obvious meanings, and this is not the place to speak extensively about that. "1 ALA taf 7:33 1 ALIsra’ 17:36 All that is intended is to make it clear that the description of Ahlus-السنة may not be given to two groups which differ completely in their way of thinking; the only one which deserves it is the one whose sayings agree with السنة النبوية; and there can be no doubt to anyone who examines these beliefs with knowledge and impartiality that those who belong to the first school (not those who make Ta‘wil) have more right to bear the aforementioned description than those of the second school (those who make Ta’wil), therefore, it is not correct to divide Ahlus-السنة into two categories; rather they are one group.
